name: azure-eventhub-py
description: Azure Event Hubs SDK for Python streaming. Use for high-throughput event ingestion, producers, consumers, and checkpointing.
capabilities: Create EventHubProducerClient to send events to Event Hub
Create EventHubConsumerClient to receive events from Event Hub
Use BlobCheckpointStore for tracking consumer progress
Implement async clients for high-throughput scenarios
Send events to specific partitions using partition keys
Get Event Hub properties and partition information
author: sickn33

The skill provides legitimate technical documentation for Azure Event Hubs, a standard cloud messaging service.
valueStatement: Build high-throughput event streaming pipelines with Azure Event Hubs Python SDK. This skill provides code patterns for producers, consumers, and checkpoint stores.
userTitle: Integrate Azure Event Hubs with Python
limitations: Requires valid Azure Event Hub namespace and credentials
Does not create or manage Event Hub resources
Does not handle application-level error recovery
Requires blob storage account for checkpoint store

useCases.title: Build event ingestion pipeline
useCases.description: Create a producer that sends high-volume events from IoT devices or applications to Azure Event Hubs

useCases.title: Process streaming events

promptTemplates.title: Send events to Event Hub
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create an EventHubProducerClient and send a batch of events using azure-eventhub Python SDK

promptTemplates.title: Receive events with checkpointing
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up an EventHubConsumerClient with BlobCheckpointStore to receive events and track progress?

promptTemplates.title: Async event streaming
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the async version of EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient for high-throughput scenarios

promptTemplates.title: Partition and routing
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I send events to specific partitions using partition_id or partition_key with azure-eventhub?

bestPractices: Use batches when sending multiple events to reduce network calls
Use checkpoint stores in production for reliable event processing
Use async clients for high-throughput scenarios
antiPatterns: Sending events one at a time without batching
Not updating checkpoints causing duplicate processing on restart
Not using context managers for client connections

faq.question: What is Azure Event Hubs?
faq.answer: Azure Event Hubs is a big data streaming platform and event ingestion service from Microsoft Azure. It can stream millions of events per second.

faq.question: What is the difference between EventHubProducerClient and EventHubConsumerClient?
faq.answer: ProducerClient sends events to Event Hub. ConsumerClient receives events from Event Hub. They are used on opposite sides of the streaming pipeline.

faq.question: What is checkpointing?
faq.answer: Checkpointing tracks the last processed event in a partition. It uses BlobCheckpointStore to persist the position so consumers can resume from where they left off.

faq.question: What is a partition key?
faq.answer: A partition key is a string that ensures all events with the same key go to the same partition. This maintains ordering for related events.

faq.question: Do I need Azure Blob Storage for Event Hubs?
faq.answer: Blob storage is required only when using checkpoint stores. For simple consumers without checkpointing, you do not need blob storage.

faq.question: What authentication does Azure Event Hubs support?
faq.answer: The SDK uses DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identities, service principals, and other Azure authentication methods.
